summary: Verified the 'refunded entry-fee' bug fix end-to-end. 6/6 pytest cases pass in /app/backend/tests/test_refund_leaderboard.py (53s). Confirmed: (1) Lone-host card_higher game with entryFee=50 -> after 20s timer the game auto-aborts, host coin balance is restored, the original -50 coin_transactions row is flagged refunded:true with refundedAt and gameId, and the host's coinsSpent on /api/leaderboard/coins-spent does NOT increase. (2) Successful host+joiner game (both pay 50) completes normally; both -50 rows stay un-refunded and both users' coinsSpent increase by >=50. (3) Injecting a synthetic refunded:true negative row for a user does not leak into the leaderboard (the {refunded: {$ne: True}} filter at /app/backend/routes/leaderboard.py L80 works). (4) Tournament size=2 with lone creator: simulated cancellation via mongo update (status=completed,winners=[]) + backend restart triggers _backfill_refunded_aborts which flags the -50 tournament spend as refunded and removes it from the leaderboard. (5) Backfill is idempotent: a second consecutive backend restart yields modified_count=0 (no log line printed, total {refunded:true} document count unchanged). (6) GET /api/leaderboard/coins-spent endpoint healthy, returns coinsSpent>=0 ints. Backend logs corroborate: startup #1 'games:2 tournaments:0', startup #2 (post lone-tournament test) 'games:0 tournaments:1', startup #3 no message.
critical_code_review_comments: add_coins(user_id, amount, type, desc, game_id=None) signature at server.py:557 cleanly threads gameId through to the coin_transactions row when provided -- backwards compatible (legacy callers pass no game_id).

_resolve_game abort branch at server.py:2502 correctly does update_many on coin_transactions BEFORE issuing the positive refund credits, so the refund credit (also tagged with gameId) is not itself marked refunded.

tournaments.py _run_tournament lone-joiner branch (L387-419) mirrors the same pattern (flag, then refund). Note: this branch only triggers when a join lands and reaches size>=size with <2 players -- which is impossible since size>=2 and creator auto-joins. The path is therefore only reachable via the startup backfill for an externally-cancelled tournament. Consider adding an explicit /api/tournaments/{tid}/cancel endpoint or a TTL job so abandoned lobbies don't sit forever consuming coins.

Leaderboard pipeline at leaderboard.py:82-93 cleanly adds {refunded: {$ne: True}} to the $match, which correctly excludes refunded:true AND keeps legacy rows where the field is missing (because {$ne: True} matches missing fields in Mongo).

Startup backfill at server.py:3422 is idempotent thanks to the {refunded: {$ne: True}} guard inside its update_many filters, AND it handles legacy rows missing gameId via a (userId,type,amount,createdAt window) fallback. Verified empirically: a 2nd consecutive restart logs nothing.

Minor: the legacy-fallback window of -5s to +60s around createdAt assumes the spend happens within 60s of the game/tournament document creation. For a tournament that sat in lobby for hours before being cancelled, only the creator's spend (at create time) is covered; any post-create joiners' spends could fall outside the window. Low impact since cancelled tournaments have <2 players, but worth noting.

The tournament 'lone-joiner cancel' code path is currently UNREACHABLE through public API (no cancel endpoint, _run_tournament only runs at size). The fix works in practice only because the startup backfill heals it on next restart. A future improvement: add an autocancel job or an explicit endpoint.
test_credentials: hosttest1780846544@test.com / TestPass123! (host, used as primary actor). scatter1@test.com / TestPass123! (joiner for completed-game regression). All identifiers use email; login is /api/auth/login with {identifier,password}.
seed_data_creation: Test transiently inserts/deletes one synthetic refunded coin_transactions row and creates one tournament 'TEST_Cancel_T' (size=2, fee=50) that is left in status=completed,winners=[] with its -50 spend now marked refunded:true. Two aborted card_higher game_sessions are also left in DB. None of this affects any leaderboard total since all spend rows involved are refunded:true.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 6 cases in /app/backend/tests/test_refund_leaderboard.py pass in ~53s. The lone-host game abort test sleeps 22s to let the 20s GAME_TIMER_SECONDS expire then GETs /api/games/{id} which triggers _check_and_resolve_if_expired. The successful-game test relies on minPlayers=2 for card_higher. The tournament-cancel test uses pymongo to simulate the cancel state then runs `sudo supervisorctl restart backend` to trigger _backfill_refunded_aborts. Test users hosttest1780846544 and scatter1 should both stay in room 6a258fd574a5efd0f4d3baf5 (World Vibez).
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE (already fixed by main agent): GET /api/leaderboard/coins-spent summed every negative coin_transactions row, including the -entryFee row for games that were later aborted+refunded. The refund credit (+entryFee) is positive and filtered out by amount<0, so the cancelled fee was effectively double-counted: it neither reduced the user's coin balance (refund credited it back) nor was discounted from the spend leaderboard. FIX: (a) add_coins now optionally tags the row with gameId; (b) host/join paths in server.py and tournaments.py pass gameId; (c) abort branches in _resolve_game and _run_tournament update_many({gameId, type, amount<0}, {$set:{refunded:true,refundedAt}}) BEFORE issuing the positive refund credit; (d) coins-spent pipeline now $match's {refunded:{$ne:True}}; (e) startup backfill heals legacy aborted games/tournaments idempotently with a (userId,type,amount,createdAt window) fallback for rows missing gameId. All five layers verified.
